name: Phishing Response
description: End-to-end response playbook for email phishing campaigns — covers header analysis, attachment/URL triage, scope assessment, and remediation.

steps.title: Email Header Analysis
steps.description: Parse email headers to determine sender authenticity (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), identify relay hops, extract originating IP, and classify delivery type as attachment or URL.

steps.title: Attachment Sandbox Detonation
steps.description: Submit the attachment hash to VirusTotal and an internal sandbox. Record VT score, family, and behaviour tags. Determine if macro execution was triggered.

steps.title: URL Reputation Check
steps.description: Query VirusTotal (via Tines) for the embedded URL / domain. Check for redirects to credential-harvesting pages or drive-by-download infrastructure.

steps.title: Privileged User Impact Check
steps.description: Review LDAP/AD for all recipients (case context). Determine if any affected accounts hold elevated privileges (DA, EA, privileged service accounts). If yes, escalate immediately to credential_theft playbook.

steps.title: Quarantine and Notify
steps.description: Pull the phishing email from all recipient inboxes via email gateway API. Block sender domain at MX and proxy. Notify affected users with security awareness guidance.

steps.title: Containment — Block & Reset
steps.description: Block malicious domain/IP at perimeter firewall and DNS sinkholes. Force password reset for all recipient accounts. Revoke active sessions in Azure AD. Preserve forensic evidence (email headers, attachment, logs).

steps.title: Document and Close
steps.description: Write final incident report. Tag all IOCs in threat intel platform. Submit campaign indicators to ISACs. Schedule post-incident review. Update detection rules to catch similar campaigns.

name: Credential Theft & Lateral Movement Response
description: Structured response for credential compromise events involving password dumping, pass-the-hash/ticket, and lateral movement across the internal network.

steps.title: Identify Compromised Account Scope
steps.description: Pull authentication logs for the past 72 hours. Identify all successful logins from unusual IPs or outside business hours. List every account showing anomalous auth patterns.

steps.title: Declare Major Incident — Crisis Management
steps.description: Escalate to CISO and senior leadership. Activate incident response retainer. Consider broad AD forest reset procedures. Isolate affected network segments.

steps.title: Targeted Account Containment
steps.description: Disable compromised accounts in AD. Force logoff all active sessions. Issue new credentials via out-of-band channel. Review account's group memberships and recent permission changes.

steps.title: Credential Dump Artefact Hunt
steps.description: Search endpoints for known credential dumping tool hashes (Mimikatz, ProcDump, WCE). Submit identified hashes to VirusTotal. Check for LSASS memory access events in EDR telemetry.

steps.title: Memory and Disk Forensics
steps.description: Acquire memory image and disk snapshot from affected endpoints. Analyse for injected code, scheduled tasks, registry run keys, and WMI subscriptions used for persistence.

steps.title: Lateral Movement Scope
steps.description: Map all SMB, RDP, WMI, and PsExec connections originating from compromised hosts. Identify pivot targets. Check for new local admin accounts created on remote hosts.

steps.title: Network Isolation
steps.description: Apply micro-segmentation ACLs to isolate compromised hosts. Block lateral movement protocols (445, 135, 5985) between user VLANs. Enable enhanced logging on all DC authentication events.

steps.title: Remediation & Hardening
steps.description: Reset KRBTGT twice (48hr gap). Enable Protected Users group for privileged accounts. Deploy LAPS. Enforce MFA on all remote access. Review and tighten delegation settings in AD.

name: Ransomware Response
description: Emergency response playbook for ransomware incidents covering initial detection, blast radius assessment, containment, recovery, and post-incident hardening.

steps.title: Initial Triage — Confirm Ransomware
steps.description: Confirm encryption activity via EDR telemetry and file system change monitoring. Identify ransomware family via ransom note and extension pattern. Submit loader hash to VirusTotal.

branches.label: Family confirmed — proceed with targeted response
Unknown family — proceed with generic response

steps.title: Emergency Containment
steps.description: Immediately isolate affected hosts at the network switch level. Kill SMB sharing on all hosts. Pause backup jobs to prevent backup encryption. Activate crisis communication tree.

steps.title: Domain Controller Recovery
steps.description: Restore DC from last known good backup (pre-encryption snapshot). Verify AD database integrity. Reset KRBTGT. Validate all trust relationships. Perform authoritative SYSVOL restore if needed.

steps.title: Backup Validation & Recovery Scoping
steps.description: Identify last clean backup snapshot for affected systems. Verify backup integrity and confirm backups are NOT encrypted. Prioritise recovery order by business criticality.

branches.label: No clean backup — ransom decision required

steps.title: Ransom Payment Decision Gate
steps.description: Legal, executive, and cyber insurance review required. Document regulatory notification obligations (GDPR 72hr, sector-specific). Engage law enforcement (FBI IC3, CISA). This step requires sign-off before proceeding.

steps.title: System Recovery & Validation
steps.description: Restore systems from clean backups. Rebuild compromised hosts from golden images. Validate data integrity. Re-enable services incrementally. Monitor for reinfection indicators during recovery.

steps.title: Post-Incident Hardening
steps.description: Implement offline backup strategy (3-2-1). Deploy EDR on all endpoints. Enable network segmentation between IT and OT. Conduct tabletop exercise within 30 days. Submit TTPs to sector ISAC.

name: Malware C2 Beacon Response
description: Response playbook for detected Command and Control beacon activity — covering C2 infrastructure analysis, host forensics, and network-level containment.

steps.title: C2 Infrastructure Identification
steps.description: Query VirusTotal and AbuseIPDB (via Tines) for the beacon destination IP. Identify associated domains, hosting ASN, and known threat infrastructure. Determine beacon protocol (HTTP, HTTPS, DNS, SMB).

steps.title: Emergency Network Block & Host Isolation
steps.description: Block C2 IP/domain at all network egress points (firewall, DNS sinkhole, proxy). Isolate beaconing host from network while preserving memory state for forensics. Alert SOC leadership.

steps.title: Enhanced Monitoring & Verification
steps.description: Enable full packet capture on the beaconing host. Monitor for additional C2 check-ins, lateral movement, and data staging. Verify the beacon pattern (jitter, timing, User-Agent) against known tool profiles.

branches.label: Beacon confirmed — escalate to high response

steps.title: Host Forensics — Implant Analysis
steps.description: Acquire memory image. Extract implant from memory or disk. Analyse configuration (C2 URLs, sleep intervals, kill date). Identify persistence mechanisms (registry, scheduled task, service, DLL hijack).

steps.title: Remediation — Remove Implant & Persistence
steps.description: Remove all identified persistence mechanisms. Delete implant files. Reimage host if rootkit or firmware-level persistence is suspected. Validate remediation with EDR re-scan.

steps.title: Threat Intel Update & Case Closure
steps.description: Add all C2 infrastructure IOCs to blocklists and TIP. Create detection rules for beacon patterns (YARA, Sigma). Share TTPs with sector ISAC. Close case with full timeline documented.

name: DDoS Mitigation Response
description: Rapid response playbook for volumetric and application-layer DDoS attacks targeting internet-facing services.

steps.title: Attack Classification & Baseline
steps.description: Identify attack vector (volumetric UDP/TCP flood, amplification, HTTP flood, SSL exhaustion). Measure traffic volume (Gbps/Mpps). Identify targeted IP and service. Determine if single-source or botnet.

steps.title: CDN & Upstream Scrubbing Activation
steps.description: Engage CDN DDoS protection (Cloudflare / Akamai / AWS Shield Advanced). Enable IP reputation-based blocking. Request BGP blackhole routing from upstream ISP if traffic exceeds 10Gbps. Increase SYN cookie threshold.

steps.title: WAF & Rate Limiting Response
steps.description: Enable WAF challenge mode. Deploy rate limiting rules based on attack signature (URI, User-Agent, ASN). Add CAPTCHA for suspicious traffic patterns. Block known DDoS-for-hire source ASNs.

steps.title: ISP & Emergency Escalation
steps.description: Contact ISP NOC for upstream nullroute or traffic diversion. Engage DDoS protection vendor emergency hotline. Consider temporary service suspension of non-critical functions to preserve capacity.

steps.title: Recovery Monitoring
steps.description: Monitor traffic normalisation over 30-minute window. Verify service availability from multiple geographic vantage points. Gradually remove emergency restrictions. Confirm no residual impact on legitimate users.

steps.title: Post-Attack Review & Hardening
steps.description: Document attack profile and peak traffic volumes. Review and adjust rate limit thresholds. Update incident response runbook. Consider always-on scrubbing if repeat attacks are likely. Submit attack IOCs to threat intel.
